# Paint Color HQ — Complete Reference

> A technical interior design and paint reference database containing 25,000+ colors across 14 major brands, with cross-brand matching powered by CIEDE2000 color science. Provides exact color metrics (Hex, RGB, LRV), undertone classification, Delta E perceptual difference scores, and free visualization tools.

## Master Color Profiles
- [Sherwin-Williams Agreeable Gray (SW 7029)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/agreeable-gray-7029): Hex #D1CBC1, RGB 209/203/193, LRV 60. America's best-selling paint color — a warm greige with balanced neutral undertones. Closest BM match: Wish AF-680 (Delta E 0.91).
- [Sherwin-Williams Alabaster (SW 7008)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/alabaster-7008): Hex #EDEAE0, RGB 237/234/224, LRV 82. A warm off-white with subtle beige undertones. Top seller for trim and whole-house white.
- [Benjamin Moore White Dove (OC-17)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/white-dove-oc-17): Hex #EDE6D3, RGB 237/230/211, LRV 83. The most popular warm white in America. Creamy without reading yellow.
- [Benjamin Moore Revere Pewter (HC-172)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/revere-pewter-hc-172): Hex #CCC7B9, RGB 204/199/185, LRV 55. A warm greige with green undertones. Closest SW match: Useful Gray 7050 (Delta E 0.88).
- [Sherwin-Williams Repose Gray (SW 7015)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/repose-gray-7015): Hex #C2BFB8, RGB 194/191/184, LRV 58. A true neutral gray — does not shift purple or blue under artificial light.
- [Benjamin Moore Hale Navy (HC-154)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/hale-navy-hc-154): Hex #3B444B, RGB 59/68/75, LRV 8. A deeply saturated, transitional navy blue for accent walls and cabinetry.
- [Sherwin-Williams Pure White (SW 7005)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/pure-white-7005): Hex #EDECE6, RGB 237/236/230, LRV 84. A warm white with a slight cream undertone. One of SW's all-time best sellers.
- [Benjamin Moore Chantilly Lace (2121-70)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/chantilly-lace-2121-70): Hex #F5F2ED, RGB 245/242/237, LRV 90. BM's cleanest true white — virtually no undertone.

## Cross-Brand Color Matching
- [Color Compare Tool](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/compare): Side-by-side comparison of any two colors with Delta E (CIEDE2000) perceptual difference score. Delta E < 1 = virtually identical, 1-2 = very close, 2-3 = close, 3-5 = noticeable difference.
- [Color Search](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/search): Search 25,000+ colors by name, hex code, brand, color family, or undertone across all 14 brands.
- [Brand-to-Brand Matches](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/match/sherwin-williams/to/benjamin-moore): Top 50 cross-brand matches between any two brands, ranked by Delta E score.

## Color Visualization & Planning Tools
- [Room Color Visualizer](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/tools/room-visualizer): Preview paint colors on walls, ceiling, trim, accent wall, and floor in a realistic room scene. Instant color swapping.
- [Photo Color Identifier](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/tools/color-identifier): Upload a photo, click any spot, instantly find the closest matching paint colors from all 14 brands using CIEDE2000.
- [Palette Generator](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/tools/palette-generator): Build complementary, analogous, triadic, and split-complementary color schemes matched to real paint colors from 14 brands.
- [Paint Calculator](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/tools/paint-calculator): Calculate exact gallons needed by room dimensions. Accounts for doors, windows, and number of coats.

## Expert Guides & Analysis
- [The 15 Best White Paint Colors (2026)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/best-white-paint-colors-guide): 15 whites compared by undertone (warm/cool/true), LRV, and best room application. Includes cross-brand equivalents and LRV data for every pick.
- [Paint Color Undertones Explained](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/understanding-paint-color-undertones): How to identify blue, green, purple, and brown undertones in gray, white, and beige paints. Explains why Repose Gray reads as a true neutral and why Revere Pewter shifts green in cool light.
- [How to Match Paint Colors Across Brands](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/how-to-find-perfect-color-match-across-brands): Step-by-step guide using CIEDE2000 Delta E scores with real examples (Agreeable Gray → Behr, BM, PPG equivalents). Delta E under 2.0 is a match most people cannot distinguish on walls.
- [Behr vs Sherwin-Williams vs Benjamin Moore (2026)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/behr-vs-sherwin-williams-vs-benjamin-moore): Price comparison ($35–90/gal), quality ratings, availability, and color selection. Includes side-by-side Delta E comparisons of top sellers.
- [Sherwin-Williams vs Benjamin Moore: Full Comparison](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/sherwin-williams-vs-benjamin-moore): Coverage rates, durability, price per sq ft, and which brand wins by use case (exteriors, cabinets, whole-house neutrals).
- [Best Bathroom Paint Colors (2026)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/best-bathroom-paint-colors): 15 moisture-resistant picks organized by mood: spa blues, warm whites, sage greens, earth tones, moody jewel tones. Includes LRV guidance for small bathrooms.
- [Best Kitchen Paint Colors (2026)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/best-kitchen-paint-colors-2025): 15 kitchen colors with finish guidance (satin/semi-gloss) and countertop pairing advice. BM Advance vs SW Emerald Urethane for cabinets.
- [Best Kitchen Colors by Sherwin-Williams](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/best-sherwin-williams-kitchen-colors): 18 SW-specific kitchen picks with LRV data, cabinet vs. wall applications, and hardware pairing recommendations.
- [Best Exterior Paint Colors (2026)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/best-exterior-paint-colors): Top picks for siding, trim, and front doors. UV resistance specs, 15+ year durability formulas.
- [Best Living Room Paint Colors (2026)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/best-living-room-paint-colors): Picks for open-plan spaces, cozy rooms, and high-ceiling great rooms, organized by light exposure and size.
- [Best Bedroom Paint Colors for Calm](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/calming-bedroom-paint-colors): Sleep-promoting picks. Bedrooms benefit from LRV 45–65 for restful atmosphere.
- [Best Behr Colors for Bedrooms](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/best-behr-colors-for-bedrooms): Budget-friendly Behr picks at $35–50/gallon. Includes Home Depot SKUs and undertone analysis.
- [Benjamin Moore's Most Popular Whites](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/benjamin-moore-most-popular-whites): BM's top-selling whites ranked by LRV: White Dove (83), Simply White (89), Chantilly Lace (90), Cloud White (85), Linen White (79).
- [Best Paint Colors for North-Facing Rooms](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/best-paint-colors-north-facing-rooms): North-facing rooms need LRV 65+ for whites. Specific picks: Navajo White (LRV 70), Antique White (LRV 72), Ivory Lace (LRV 77).
- [Best Paint Colors for East-Facing Rooms](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/best-paint-colors-east-facing-rooms): Best colors for rooms with warm morning light that shifts cool by afternoon.
- [Warm vs. Cool Paint Colors: Complete Guide](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/warm-vs-cool-paint-colors): Repose Gray = true neutral; Agreeable Gray = warm-neutral; Stonington Gray = cool.
- [2026 Colors of the Year: Every Brand Compared](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/2026-colors-of-the-year-every-brand-compared): SW Universal Khaki (LRV 42), BM Silhouette (dark gray-brown), Behr Hidden Gem (jade, LRV ~16), PPG Warm Mahogany (brown-red), Valspar Warm Eucalyptus (gray-green).
- [2026 Paint Color Trends](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/paint-color-trends-2026): Green and earth tones dominate. 34% YoY search growth for "sage green walls."
- [Paint Sheen Guide](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/paint-sheen-guide): Flat for ceilings, eggshell for living rooms, satin for kitchens/bathrooms, semi-gloss for trim/doors.
- [How to Test Paint Samples](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/how-to-test-paint-samples): Test on 12"×12" boards viewed in actual room lighting at different times of day — not on tiny paint chips.

## Browse by Brand
- [All 1,527 Sherwin-Williams Colors](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/brands/sherwin-williams): America's largest paint company. Top sellers: Agreeable Gray, Pure White, Alabaster.
- [All Benjamin Moore Colors](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/brands/benjamin-moore): Premium paint with ~3,500 colors. Top sellers: White Dove, Revere Pewter, Chantilly Lace.
- [All Behr Colors](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/brands/behr): Home Depot exclusive. Best value at $35-50/gallon. Top sellers: Silver Drop, Ultra Pure White.
- [All PPG Colors](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/brands/ppg)
- [All Valspar Colors](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/brands/valspar): Lowe's exclusive. $30-55/gallon.
- [All Farrow & Ball Colors](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/brands/farrow-ball): Ultra-premium British brand. $100-120/gallon. Known for rich, complex pigments.
- [All Dunn-Edwards Colors](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/brands/dunn-edwards): Premium West Coast brand. Zero-VOC formulas.

## Color Science Methodology
- [About Our Methodology](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/about): Uses CIEDE2000 — the International Commission on Illumination (CIE) standard for perceptual color difference. Every color includes hex, RGB, LRV, undertone classification (warm/cool/neutral), and cross-brand matches ranked by Delta E score.

## Common Questions This Site Answers
- What is the Benjamin Moore equivalent of Sherwin-Williams Agreeable Gray? → Wish AF-680 (Delta E 0.91) — virtually identical on walls.
- What is the Behr equivalent of Agreeable Gray? → Toasty Gray N320-2 (Delta E 1.4) — available at Home Depot.
- What undertone does Agreeable Gray have? → Warm balanced neutral — no single undertone dominates.
- What is the best white paint for trim? → Chantilly Lace (true white, LRV 90) or White Dove (warm white, LRV 83).
- What is the best white paint for cabinets? → Simply White (2143-70, LRV 89) for warm kitchens; Chantilly Lace (2121-70, LRV 90) for modern kitchens.
- What is the difference between Repose Gray and Agreeable Gray? → Repose Gray (LRV 58) = true neutral; Agreeable Gray (LRV 60) = warm greige.
- What LRV should I use for a north-facing room? → LRV 65+ for whites and light neutrals. Avoid cool-undertone grays.
- How do I match paint colors across brands? → Use CIEDE2000 Delta E scores. Delta E < 2.0 = most people cannot tell on walls.
- What is the hex code for Agreeable Gray? → #D1CBC1 (RGB 209/203/193, LRV 60)
- What does LRV mean? → Light Reflectance Value (0–100). LRV > 50 makes rooms feel open; LRV > 70 = light color.
- What is the 2026 Sherwin-Williams Color of the Year? → Universal Khaki (SW 6150), Hex #B8A992, LRV 42.
- What is the 2026 Benjamin Moore Color of the Year? → Silhouette AF-655, a dark gray-brown from the Affinity collection.

---

# About Paint Color HQ
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/about

## What is Paint Color HQ?

Paint Color HQ is a paint color discovery and cross-brand matching platform. We catalog **over 25,000 colors across 14 major paint brands** and provide free tools that help homeowners, interior designers, and painting professionals choose paint colors with confidence.

Our mission is straightforward: eliminate the guesswork from choosing paint colors. Instead of buying dozens of sample pots or wondering whether a Sherwin-Williams shade has a Benjamin Moore equivalent, you can find answers instantly with data you can trust.

### Free Tools

Every tool on Paint Color HQ is free to use, with no account required.

- **[Room Visualizer](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/tools/room-visualizer)** — Preview colors on walls, trim, and floors in a realistic room scene.
- **[Color Identifier](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/tools/color-identifier)** — Upload a photo, click any spot, and find matching paint colors.
- **[Palette Generator](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/tools/palette-generator)** — Build coordinated palettes using color harmony rules mapped to real paints.
- **[Paint Calculator](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/tools/paint-calculator)** — Estimate gallons needed based on room dimensions, doors, and windows.
- **[Compare Tool](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/compare)** — Place two or more colors side by side with Delta E scores.
- **[Color Search](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/search)** — Search by name, code, hex value, or browse by color family and brand.

## Our Color Science Methodology

Color matching on Paint Color HQ is powered by **CIEDE2000**, the latest international standard for measuring perceptual color difference. Unlike older formulas (CIE76, CIE94), CIEDE2000 accounts for how the human eye actually perceives differences in lightness, chroma, and hue, making it the most accurate method available for comparing paint colors digitally.

For every color in our database, we compute the perceptual distance — called **Delta E** — to every color from every other brand. This score tells you exactly how close two colors are to the human eye:

| Delta E Range | What It Means |
|---|---|
| < 1.0 | Virtually identical — the difference is imperceptible to most people |
| 1.0 - 2.0 | Very close — most people cannot tell them apart |
| 2.0 - 3.0 | Close — a slight difference may be visible when placed side by side |
| 3.0 - 5.0 | Noticeable difference — clearly distinguishable to most observers |

### Color Data We Provide

Every color page on Paint Color HQ includes:

- **Hex and RGB values** — the exact digital color representation
- **LRV (Light Reflectance Value)** — how much light a color reflects, useful for choosing colors for different room sizes and lighting conditions
- **Undertone classification** — whether a color reads warm, cool, or neutral, which affects how it pairs with other elements in your space
- **Cross-brand matches** — the closest equivalent colors from every other brand, ranked by Delta E accuracy

## How We're Different

Paint Color HQ is not a paint store. We don't sell paint, and we're not affiliated with any paint manufacturer. We're an **independent reference tool** built to help you make informed decisions about paint colors before you visit the store.

1. **Cross-Brand Matching** — Paint brands don't publish cross-references to each other's colors. We compute matches across all 14 brands using CIEDE2000, so you can find an equivalent color no matter which brand your local store carries.

2. **Data-Driven, Not Opinion-Based** — Every color recommendation on this site is backed by color science. When we say two colors are close, we show you the Delta E score to prove it. No subjective "these look similar" guesses.

3. **Completely Free** — All tools, color data, and matching results are free to use with no account or sign-up required. We believe paint color information should be accessible to everyone.

4. **Accuracy Transparency** — All color data is based on digital hex and RGB values published by each manufacturer. We update our database regularly and always recommend verifying with physical paint samples, because real paint looks different depending on finish, lighting, coats, and primer.

## Brands We Cover

Our database includes colors from 14 major paint brands available in North America and internationally:

- Sherwin-Williams
- Benjamin Moore
- Behr
- PPG
- Valspar
- Dunn-Edwards
- Farrow & Ball
- Clark+Kensington
- Glidden
- Olympic
- Dutch Boy

We add new brands and update existing color data regularly. If you notice a missing color or incorrect data, please [let us know](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/contact).

# Blog Posts

## 2026 Colors of the Year: Every Major Brand Compared
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/2026-colors-of-the-year-every-brand-compared
Date: 2026-01-08 | Author: Paint Color HQ Staff
*See every major paint brand's 2026 Color of the Year side by side — from earthy greens to warm neutrals — with closest cross-brand matches.*

Every year, the biggest paint brands announce their Color of the Year — a single shade they believe captures the cultural mood. For 2026, the picks are strikingly aligned: earthy greens, warm neutrals, and nature-inspired tones dominate. Let's break down every major selection and find the closest matches across brands.

## Sherwin-Williams: Universal Khaki

[**Universal Khaki** (Sherwin-Williams, #b8a992)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/universal-khaki-6150) is a warm, sandy neutral with golden undertones. Sherwin-Williams chose this versatile shade as their 2026 pick — it bridges the gap between beige and gray, making it one of the most livable neutrals in their palette. With an LRV of 42, it works as a whole-house color in both traditional and modern settings.

**Closest cross-brand matches:** Benjamin Moore Weimaraner AF-155 (Delta E 1.2), Behr Rustic Taupe N210-4 (Delta E 1.8), PPG Kangaroo PPG1078-4 (Delta E 2.1). Use our [color compare tool](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/compare) to check any of these matches side-by-side.

## Benjamin Moore: Silhouette

[**Silhouette** (Benjamin Moore, #58514d)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/silhouette-af-655) is a deep, warm charcoal with brown-gray undertones. Benjamin Moore went darker than most brands for 2026, positioning this color as a statement shade for accent walls, cabinetry, and exterior doors.

**Closest cross-brand matches:** Sherwin-Williams Porpoise SW 7047 (Delta E 1.5), Behr Silhouette N170-7 (Delta E 2.0), PPG Bark PPG1007-7 (Delta E 2.3).

## Behr: Hidden Gem

[**Hidden Gem** (Behr, #596D69)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/behr/hidden-gem-n430-6a) is a smoky jade green with gray undertones. It captures the moody, nature-inspired aesthetic that has been building for two years. At LRV 15, this is a bold choice best suited for accent walls, front doors, or powder rooms.

**Closest cross-brand matches:** Benjamin Moore Jack Pine 692 (Delta E 1.9), Sherwin-Williams Basil SW 6194 (Delta E 2.4), Farrow & Ball Green Smoke No.47 (Delta E 3.1). Browse the full [green color family](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/family/green).

## Valspar: Warm Eucalyptus

[**Warm Eucalyptus** (Valspar, #7C7F70)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/valspar/warm-eucalyptus-8004-28f) is a grounded gray-green that nods to the same earthy trend as Behr's pick, but in a softer, more livable range. At LRV 25, it works as a whole-room color in medium to large spaces.

**Closest cross-brand matches:** Benjamin Moore Rosemary Sprig 2144-30 (Delta E 1.7), Sherwin-Williams Retreat SW 6207 (Delta E 2.0), Behr Conifer Green PPU12-18 (Delta E 2.5). See all [Valspar colors](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/brands/valspar).

## PPG: Warm Mahogany

[**Warm Mahogany** (PPG, #6d4741)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/ppg/warm-mahogany-1060-7) is a rich, earthy brown-red — the warmest and most saturated pick of the year. PPG is betting that homeowners are ready for deeper color commitments beyond neutrals. It works beautifully on front doors, accent walls, and built-in cabinetry.

**Closest cross-brand matches:** Benjamin Moore Freshly Roasted CW-270 (Delta E 1.4), Sherwin-Williams Fiery Brown SW 6055 (Delta E 1.9), Behr Cinnamon Brandy PPU2-18 (Delta E 2.6). Explore the [brown color family](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/family/brown).

## What the 2026 Picks Tell Us

Three themes are unmistakable: earth tones are in (every pick has brown, green, or golden undertones), cool gray is officially out (zero brands picked a blue-gray), and neutrals are getting warmer (even the "neutral" picks like Universal Khaki lean golden rather than ashy).

For homeowners, the takeaway is clear: if you've been living with cool gray walls from 2018, the industry has moved on. The new neutral is warm — think sandy beiges, terracotta tans, and olive greens. Use our [room visualizer](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/tools/room-visualizer) to preview any of these 2026 Colors of the Year on your walls. For a deeper look at the trends shaping 2026, see our [paint color trends 2026](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/paint-color-trends-2026) guide.

---

## 2025 Colors of the Year: Every Brand Compared
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/2025-colors-of-the-year-every-brand-compared
Date: 2025-09-10 | Author: Paint Color HQ Staff
*A look back at every major brand's 2025 Color of the Year — what they got right, what resonated with homeowners, and how to find the closest match from any brand.*

The 2025 Colors of the Year marked a turning point in interior design: every major brand leaned into warm, earthy palettes and moved away from the cool grays that defined the late 2010s. Here's a recap of every pick and cross-brand matches.

## Sherwin-Williams: Drift of Mist

[**Drift of Mist** (Sherwin-Williams, #E1DDD3)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/drift-of-mist-9166) is a warm off-white with soft green undertones. With an LRV of 75, it was positioned as the ultimate whole-house neutral for open-concept living.

## Benjamin Moore: Cinnamon Slate

[**Cinnamon Slate** (Benjamin Moore, #4B3C33)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/cinnamon-slate-2113-20) is a rich, earthy brown that set the tone for the warm revolution. Benjamin Moore went bold with this pick, signaling that neutrals could have real depth.

## Behr: Rumors

[**Rumors** (Behr, #6B5C5A)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/behr/rumors-n130-6) is a warm taupe-brown that bridged the gap between gray and brown. It resonated strongly with homeowners looking to update from cool gray without committing to full color.

Browse the [beige color family](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/family/beige) for similar warm neutrals.

---

## Understanding Paint Color Undertones
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/understanding-paint-color-undertones
Date: 2025-08-21 | Author: Paint Color HQ Staff
*Master the hidden hues that make or break your paint choice. This guide explains what undertones are, how to spot them, and how to avoid the dreaded "this gray looks purple" mistake.*

Undertones are the #1 reason paint colors "look different on the wall." Every color — even whites, grays, and beiges — has an underlying hue that becomes more visible once it covers a large surface. Understanding undertones is the single most important skill for choosing paint colors that work in your space. Every color page on Paint Color HQ shows its undertone classification — use our [color search](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/search) to filter by warm, cool, or neutral.

## What Are Undertones?

Think of a paint color as having two layers. The **surface color** is what you notice first — "gray," "white," "beige." The **undertone** is the subtle hue hiding underneath. A gray might have blue, green, purple, or brown undertones. A white might lean yellow, pink, or blue. These undertones are invisible on a tiny paint chip but become unmistakable on a full wall.

## The Most Common Undertones in Popular Colors

**Gray undertones:** Most grays lean blue (like [**Stonington Gray** (Benjamin Moore, #B0B7BB)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/stonington-gray-hc-170)), green (like [**Revere Pewter** (Benjamin Moore, #CCC7B9)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/revere-pewter-hc-172)), or purple (like [**Passive** (Sherwin-Williams, #C2BFBB)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/passive-sw-7064)). The rarest and most sought-after grays are "true neutrals" with balanced undertones, like [**Repose Gray** (Sherwin-Williams, #C2BFB8)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/repose-gray-7015).

**White undertones:** Warm whites lean yellow or cream (like [**White Dove** (Benjamin Moore, #EDE6D3)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/white-dove-oc-17)). Cool whites lean blue or gray (like [**Decorator's White** (Benjamin Moore, #EEF0EC)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/decorators-white-oc-149)). True whites have virtually no undertone (like [**Chantilly Lace** (Benjamin Moore, #F5F1EB)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/chantilly-lace-2121-70)). See our [white color family](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/family/white) and [off-white family](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/family/off-white).

**Beige undertones:** Beiges can lean yellow, pink, or green. Yellow-beiges feel warm and sunny. Pink-beiges feel cozy but can clash with orange-toned wood. Green-beiges (greiges) are the most versatile — [**Agreeable Gray** (Sherwin-Williams, #D1CBC1)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/agreeable-gray-7029) is the poster child.

## How to Identify Undertones

**Hold the color next to pure white.** Put a paint chip next to a sheet of bright white printer paper. The undertone jumps out immediately — you'll see the yellow, blue, green, or pink that's hiding in the color.

**Compare colors within the same family.** Put three "grays" side by side. One will look blue, one green, one purple. That's the undertone.

**Check the color in your actual lighting.** North-facing light amplifies cool (blue) undertones. South-facing light amplifies warm (yellow) undertones. East-facing rooms shift from warm morning to cool afternoon. Test samples in the room you'll actually be painting. Use our [room visualizer](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/tools/room-visualizer) for a quick digital preview, then buy physical samples.

## Why Undertones Matter for Your Room

Undertones need to match or complement the fixed elements in your room: flooring, countertops, tiles, and large furniture. A warm-toned hardwood floor + a cool blue-gray wall = visual tension. A warm-toned floor + a warm greige wall = harmony. Use our [compare tool](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/compare) to put two colors side by side and see if their undertones harmonize.

---

## How to Find the Perfect Color Match Across Paint Brands
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/how-to-find-perfect-color-match-across-brands
Date: 2025-09-05 | Author: Paint Color HQ Staff
*Love a color from one brand but need it from another? Here's how to find the closest cross-brand match using CIEDE2000 science — with real examples and Delta E scores.*

When you find a paint color you love, it often comes from a brand that's not convenient for you. Maybe your painter only works with Sherwin-Williams, but the color you fell in love with is Benjamin Moore. Maybe you want a Farrow & Ball look without the $100/gallon price tag. Cross-brand matching solves this — and it's what Paint Color HQ was built to do.

## What Is Cross-Brand Color Matching?

Cross-brand matching finds the closest equivalent color from one brand in another brand's palette. Every paint brand uses its own proprietary color formulas, so no two brands have identical colors. But many are extremely close — close enough that you can't tell the difference on a wall.

The key is measuring "close enough" with science, not guesswork. Paint Color HQ uses the CIEDE2000 formula to calculate the perceptual difference (Delta E) between every pair of colors across all 14 brands. A Delta E under 2.0 means the colors are virtually indistinguishable to the human eye.

## Real-World Example: Matching Agreeable Gray

[**Agreeable Gray** (Sherwin-Williams, #D1CBC1)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/agreeable-gray-7029) is the best-selling paint color in America. Here are the closest matches from other brands:

- Benjamin Moore Wish AF-680: Delta E 0.91 (virtually identical)
- Behr Campfire Ash N220-2: Delta E 1.4 (very close)
- PPG Whiskers PPG1025-3: Delta E 1.6 (very close)
- Valspar Gravity 4005-1B: Delta E 1.8 (very close)
- Farrow & Ball Elephant's Breath No.229: Delta E 3.2 (noticeable difference)

## How to Find Matches on Paint Color HQ

1. Search for your color by name, brand, or hex code using [color search](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/search)
2. On the color page, scroll to "Cross-Brand Matches" to see the closest equivalents from all 14 brands, ranked by Delta E
3. Use the [compare tool](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/compare) to see any two colors side by side
4. Preview your match in a room with the [room visualizer](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/tools/room-visualizer)

## Tips for the Best Cross-Brand Match

**Always sample both colors.** Even a Delta E under 1.0 doesn't guarantee they'll look identical on your specific wall with your specific lighting. Digital matching gets you 95% of the way there — physical samples confirm the last 5%.

**Check the undertone, not just the overall shade.** Two colors can have the same Delta E score but different undertones. One might lean warm while the other leans cool. Our color pages show the undertone classification for every color. Read our [undertone guide](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/understanding-paint-color-undertones) for more.

**Consider the finish.** The same color in matte vs. satin looks different. If you're switching brands, try to match the sheen level as well.

---

## Best White Paint Colors Guide
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/best-white-paint-colors-guide
Date: 2025-09-15 | Author: Paint Color HQ Staff
*The definitive guide to choosing white paint — 15 whites ranked by undertone, LRV, and best use, with cross-brand equivalents for every pick.*

White is the most popular paint color category in America — and the hardest to get right. What looks like "just white" on a chip can read yellow, pink, blue, or gray on a full wall. This guide breaks down the 15 most popular white paints by undertone so you can pick the right one without the guesswork. Use our [room visualizer](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/tools/room-visualizer) to preview any white on your walls, and the [compare tool](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/compare) to see them side by side.

## Warm Whites

Warm whites have yellow, cream, or peach undertones. They feel cozy, inviting, and pair beautifully with natural wood, warm metals, and earth tones.

[**White Dove** (Benjamin Moore, #EDE6D3)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/white-dove-oc-17) — LRV 83. The most popular warm white in America. Creamy without reading yellow. Best for: cabinets, trim, whole-house color. SW equivalent: Pure White (SW 7005).

[**Alabaster** (Sherwin-Williams, #EDEAE0)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/alabaster-7008) — LRV 82. A soft, buttery white with the faintest beige undertone. Best for: open-concept homes, warm trim. BM equivalent: White Dove OC-17.

[**Simply White** (Benjamin Moore, #F1EDE3)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/simply-white-2143-70) — LRV 89. The bridge between warm and true white. Just a whisper of warmth. Best for: kitchens, bathrooms, transitional style.

[**Pure White** (Sherwin-Williams, #EDECE6)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/pure-white-7005) — LRV 84. Despite the name, it's a warm white with a slight cream lean. Best for: trim, cabinets, farmhouse aesthetic.

[**Cameo White** (Behr, #F0E8D8)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/behr/cameo-white-mq3-32) — A warm white with subtle cream undertones from Behr's lineup. Excellent value option.

## Cool Whites

Cool whites have blue, gray, or green undertones. They feel crisp, clean, modern, and pair well with cool-toned decor and contemporary design.

[**Decorator's White** (Benjamin Moore, #EEF0EC)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/decorators-white-oc-149) — A cool white with a subtle blue-gray undertone. Reads as fresh and modern. Best for: contemporary spaces, bathrooms with cool tile.

[**Snowbound** (Sherwin-Williams)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/snowbound-7004) — A soft cool white that pairs well with gray and blue elements.

## True Whites

True whites have minimal undertone — they read as neutral white in most lighting conditions.

[**Chantilly Lace** (Benjamin Moore, #F5F1EB)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/chantilly-lace-2121-70) — LRV 90. The cleanest, truest white. Virtually zero undertone. Best for: modern interiors, trim against colored walls, ceilings, exteriors.

[**Ultra Pure White** (Behr, #F2ECE0)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/behr/ultra-pure-white-50) — Behr's cleanest white with virtually no undertone.

## How to Choose the Right White

**Start with your fixed elements.** Warm countertops and wood tones -> warm whites. Cool tile and gray accents -> cool whites. Mixed elements -> true whites like Simply White.

**Consider your lighting.** North-facing rooms need warmer whites (White Dove, Alabaster) to counteract blue light. South-facing rooms can handle any white. East and west rooms shift throughout the day — test samples at multiple times.

**Wall white vs. trim white.** Many designers use one white for walls and a crisper white for trim. Classic combo: White Dove walls + Chantilly Lace trim.

Browse the full [white color family](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/family/white) and [off-white color family](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/family/off-white) to see every white from all 14 brands.

---

## Behr vs Sherwin-Williams vs Benjamin Moore: Which Paint Brand Is Best?
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/behr-vs-sherwin-williams-vs-benjamin-moore
Date: 2025-11-05 | Author: Paint Color HQ Staff
*The big three paint brands compared on price, quality, color accuracy, availability, and best use cases — so you can pick the right brand for your project.*

Behr, Sherwin-Williams, and Benjamin Moore control the vast majority of the residential paint market. All three make good paint — but they differ significantly in price, availability, color depth, and ideal use case.

## Price Comparison

| Brand | Budget Line | Premium Line | Where to Buy |
|---|---|---|---|
| Behr | Premium Plus ($28-32/gal) | Dynasty ($45-55/gal) | Home Depot |
| Sherwin-Williams | SuperPaint ($60-70/gal) | Emerald ($75-85/gal) | SW stores, Lowe's |
| Benjamin Moore | Ben ($50-60/gal) | Aura ($80-90/gal) | Independent dealers |

**Important:** Sherwin-Williams runs 30-40% off sales 4-6 times per year, which can drop Emerald to $50-60/gallon — making it competitive with Behr's premium offerings.

## Quality

All three brands make excellent paint at their premium tiers. In blind tests, Consumer Reports has consistently rated Behr Marquee/Dynasty at or near the top. Benjamin Moore Aura has the richest color depth and best one-coat coverage. Sherwin-Williams Emerald has the best workability — painters love how it flows off the brush.

**For cabinets and trim:** Benjamin Moore Advance (waterborne alkyd) is widely considered the best option. It self-levels to a smooth, factory-like finish.

**For walls:** Any premium line from any of these three brands will perform well. The differences are marginal.

## Color Selection

Benjamin Moore: ~3,500 colors — the largest palette. Especially deep in whites and neutrals. Sherwin-Williams: ~1,700 colors — more curated but covers every need. Behr: ~3,000+ colors — extensive range available at Home Depot.

## Availability

Behr: Home Depot (open evenings and weekends, widely accessible). Sherwin-Williams: 4,000+ company stores plus Lowe's. Benjamin Moore: Independent paint stores only (limited hours, fewer locations but expert staff).

## The Verdict

**Budget-conscious DIY:** Behr. Best value, most accessible, and DIY-friendly formulas. **Best all-around with sales:** Sherwin-Williams. Wait for a 30-40% sale and get premium paint at mid-tier prices. **Color-critical projects:** Benjamin Moore. The richest color depth and best cabinet paint (Advance). **Hiring a painter:** Sherwin-Williams or Benjamin Moore. Most contractors have SW or BM accounts with trade discounts.

## Can You Mix Brands?

Absolutely. Many designers specify Benjamin Moore colors but have them mixed in Sherwin-Williams paint (or vice versa) to get the best of both worlds. Just bring the color formula or hex code to any paint store and they can custom-match it. You can also use our [color compare tool](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/compare) to verify how close the match is before committing.

---

## 10 Calming Bedroom Colors Designers Love
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/calming-bedroom-paint-colors
Date: 2026-01-15 | Author: Paint Color HQ Staff
*Create a serene retreat with these designer-approved bedroom paint colors — soft blues, warm neutrals, and muted greens that promote relaxation.*

The best bedroom paint colors share a common trait: they're low-saturation, meaning they're muted and grayed-out rather than bright or vivid. Research shows that muted blues, greens, and warm neutrals promote relaxation and better sleep. Here are 10 designer-approved bedroom colors.

## Soft Blues

[**Quiet Moments** (Benjamin Moore, #8BA7B0)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/quiet-moments-1563) — A blue-green gray that shifts beautifully with changing light. Reads as a calming spa blue in bedrooms.

[**Sleepy Blue** (Sherwin-Williams, #B8CAD0)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/sleepy-blue-6225) — A dusty powder blue with just enough gray to prevent it from going cold. Perfect for bedrooms.

[**Light French Gray** (Behr, #B5C4CB)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/behr/light-french-gray-720e-2) — Despite the name, this is actually a soft blue-gray and one of Behr's all-time top sellers. It reads as a sophisticated blue in north-facing rooms.

## Muted Greens

[**Saybrook Sage** (Benjamin Moore, #B2BAA4)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/saybrook-sage-hc-114) — A dusty sage that glows golden in morning light and settles into a serene green by afternoon.

[**Softened Green** (Sherwin-Williams, #C2C5B4)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/softened-green-6177) — Barely green, almost gray. This whisper of color adapts to changing light without ever looking wrong.

## Warm Neutrals

[**Edgecomb Gray** (Benjamin Moore, #D3CBBA)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/edgecomb-gray-hc-173) — A warm greige with sandy undertones that feels cozy without being dark.

[**Accessible Beige** (Sherwin-Williams, #C4A882)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/accessible-beige-7036) — A true warm beige that holds its warmth even in cool light.

[**Silver Drop** (Behr, #D2CBBD)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/behr/silver-drop-790c-2) — Behr's answer to Agreeable Gray. A warm gray-beige that creates a cozy, enveloping bedroom.

Browse the [blue color family](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/family/blue) and [green color family](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/family/green) for more calming bedroom options. Use the [palette generator](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/tools/palette-generator) to build a complete bedroom scheme.

---

## Best Kitchen Paint Colors (2025-2026)
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/best-kitchen-paint-colors-2025
Date: 2025-12-03 | Author: Paint Color HQ Staff
*15 kitchen colors that work with every countertop and cabinet style — organized by mood with LRV values and cross-brand alternatives.*

Kitchens demand colors that work with multiple fixed elements — countertops, cabinets, backsplash, appliances, and flooring. The best kitchen paint colors are versatile enough to harmonize with all of these while still creating the atmosphere you want.

## Clean Whites for Kitchens

[**Chantilly Lace** (Benjamin Moore, #F5F1EB)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/chantilly-lace-2121-70) — The cleanest true white. Pairs with everything. LRV 90.

[**Alabaster** (Sherwin-Williams, #EDEAE0)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/alabaster-7008) — Warm enough to not feel sterile. The most popular white for open kitchens. LRV 82.

## Warm Neutrals for Kitchens

[**Agreeable Gray** (Sherwin-Williams, #D1CBC1)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/agreeable-gray-7029) — The best-selling paint color in America for a reason. It works with warm and cool kitchen elements alike.

[**Revere Pewter** (Benjamin Moore, #CCC7B9)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/revere-pewter-hc-172) — A warm greige that grounds open kitchens without making them feel dark.

## Greens for Kitchens

Sage and olive greens have become the most requested kitchen colors. They pair naturally with wood cabinets, marble countertops, and brass hardware.

## Blues for Kitchens

Navy blue cabinetry paired with white walls is one of the most enduring kitchen trends. [**Hale Navy** (Benjamin Moore, #3B444B)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/hale-navy-hc-154) is the go-to for kitchen islands and lower cabinets.

Use the [paint calculator](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/tools/paint-calculator) to figure out exactly how many gallons you need for your kitchen.

## Best Bathroom Paint Colors
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/best-bathroom-paint-colors
Date: 2025-12-10 | Author: Paint Color HQ Staff
*15 moisture-resistant bathroom paint colors organized by mood — spa blues, warm whites, sage greens, earth tones, and moody jewel tones.*

Bathrooms need colors that can handle humidity, look good in both natural and artificial light, and complement tile, fixtures, and hardware. These 15 colors meet all three criteria.

## Spa Blues

[**Quiet Moments** (Benjamin Moore, #8BA7B0)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/quiet-moments-1563) — The quintessential spa bathroom color. Blue-green-gray that reads tranquil in any light.

[**Sea Salt** (Sherwin-Williams)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/sea-salt-6204) — A green-tinted gray-blue that shifts between blue and green depending on lighting. One of SW's top sellers for bathrooms.

## Warm Whites for Bathrooms

[**Simply White** (Benjamin Moore, #F1EDE3)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/simply-white-2143-70) — Clean but warm. Perfect for bathrooms with cool-toned tile.

[**Pure White** (Sherwin-Williams, #EDECE6)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/pure-white-7005) — Warm enough to prevent the clinical look that pure whites can create in small bathrooms.

## Finish Recommendations

Bathrooms need moisture-resistant finishes. Use **satin** for walls (easy to clean, moisture-resistant) and **semi-gloss** for trim and doors. Avoid flat/matte finishes in bathrooms — moisture will damage them.

## The Most Popular Paint Colors of 2025
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/most-popular-paint-colors-2025
Date: 2025-10-09 | Author: Paint Color HQ Staff
*The best-selling paint colors of 2025 across all major brands — with hex codes, undertone analysis, and cross-brand matches.*

Based on sales data and trending searches, these are the most popular paint colors of 2025 across all major brands.

[**Agreeable Gray** (Sherwin-Williams, #D1CBC1)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/agreeable-gray-7029) remains the #1 selling paint color in America for the fifth year running. Its balanced warm undertone works in any room, any lighting, and with any decor style.

[**White Dove** (Benjamin Moore, #EDE6D3)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/white-dove-oc-17) holds its position as the most popular white. Interior designers continue to specify it for cabinets, trim, and whole-house color.

[**Silver Drop** (Behr, #D2CBBD)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/behr/silver-drop-790c-2) is Behr's #1 seller — a warm greige that appeals to DIYers looking for an easy, foolproof neutral.

The biggest trend shift: warm neutrals are overtaking cool grays. Colors with yellow, sand, and golden undertones are rising, while blue-grays and cool grays are declining. Browse the [beige color family](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/family/beige) for the trending warm neutrals.

---

## Warm vs Cool Paint Colors: How to Choose
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/warm-vs-cool-paint-colors
Date: 2025-10-02 | Author: Paint Color HQ Staff
*Learn the difference between warm and cool paint colors, how lighting affects temperature, and how to build a cohesive palette that flows room to room.*

The warm-vs-cool distinction is the single most important concept in choosing paint colors. Get it right, and your whole home feels cohesive. Get it wrong, and individual rooms will feel disconnected — or worse, your "gray" will look purple.

## What Makes a Color Warm or Cool?

The color wheel is divided into two halves. **Warm colors** — reds, oranges, yellows, and their derivatives — evoke sunlight, earth, and fire. **Cool colors** — blues, greens, purples — evoke water, sky, and shadow. But every color exists on a warm-to-cool spectrum.

A *warm gray* like [**Edgecomb Gray** (Benjamin Moore, #D3CBBA)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/edgecomb-gray-hc-173) has yellow-brown undertones. A *cool gray* like [**Stonington Gray** (Benjamin Moore, #B0B7BB)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/stonington-gray-hc-170) has blue undertones. Both are "gray," but they create completely different moods.

## How Lighting Affects Temperature

**North-facing rooms** get cool, blue-toned light all day. Warm colors counteract this. Cool colors get amplified and can feel cold.

**South-facing rooms** get warm, golden light. Most colors look their truest here. Cool colors work well because the warm light balances them.

**East-facing rooms** shift from warm morning light to cool afternoon shadow. Choose balanced colors that look good in both.

**West-facing rooms** get intense warm light in the afternoon. Cool colors provide balance; warm colors may feel overwhelming at sunset.

## The Connected Rooms Rule

The single most important design principle: **connected spaces should share the same temperature.** A warm living room flowing into a cool kitchen feels disjointed. Closed rooms (powder rooms, bedrooms, home offices) can break this rule freely.

Use our [room visualizer](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/tools/room-visualizer) to preview colors, and the [palette generator](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/tools/palette-generator) to build coordinated warm or cool schemes.

---

## Best Sherwin-Williams Alternatives to Benjamin Moore's Top Colors
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/best-sherwin-williams-alternatives-to-benjamin-moore
Date: 2025-11-20 | Author: Paint Color HQ Staff
*For every popular Benjamin Moore color, there's a Sherwin-Williams equivalent that's close, more accessible, and often cheaper. Here are the best cross-brand matches.*

Many homeowners love Benjamin Moore colors but find it inconvenient to shop at independent dealers. Sherwin-Williams has over 4,000 stores and runs frequent 30-40% off sales. Here are the best SW alternatives for BM's most popular colors.

**White Dove (OC-17)** -> **Pure White (SW 7005)**: Both warm whites with creamy undertones. Delta E: 2.1 — close enough for most applications.

**Revere Pewter (HC-172)** -> **Useful Gray (SW 7050)**: Delta E 0.88 — virtually identical. The closest cross-brand match in our database.

**Chantilly Lace (OC-65)** -> **High Reflective White (SW 7757)**: Both true whites with minimal undertone.

**Hale Navy (HC-154)** -> **Naval (SW 6244)**: America's two most popular navy blues. Delta E: 2.8 — noticeably different up close but similar overall.

**Simply White (OC-117)** -> **Extra White (SW 7006)**: Both slightly warm whites.

Use the [compare tool](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/compare) to see any two colors side by side with Delta E scores.

---

## Best Living Room Paint Colors
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/best-living-room-paint-colors
Date: 2025-12-17 | Author: Paint Color HQ Staff
*The best paint colors for living rooms in 2025-2026 — from timeless neutrals to trending greens, organized by style with cross-brand alternatives.*

Living rooms need colors that work with multiple furniture styles, handle changing light throughout the day, and create the right atmosphere for both entertaining and relaxing.

## Timeless Neutrals

[**Agreeable Gray** (Sherwin-Williams, #D1CBC1)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/agreeable-gray-7029) — The safest living room pick. Works with everything.

[**Revere Pewter** (Benjamin Moore, #CCC7B9)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/revere-pewter-hc-172) — A warm greige that grounds a room without making it feel dark.

[**Edgecomb Gray** (Benjamin Moore, #D3CBBA)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/edgecomb-gray-hc-173) — Sandy, warm, and sophisticated. The designer's greige.

## Trending Greens

[**Saybrook Sage** (Benjamin Moore, #B2BAA4)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/saybrook-sage-hc-114) — The sage green that launched a thousand living rooms.

[**Evergreen Fog** (Sherwin-Williams)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/evergreen-fog-9130) — SW's 2022 COTY that remains a top seller.

## Accent Wall Colors

[**Hale Navy** (Benjamin Moore, #3B444B)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/hale-navy-hc-154) — The quintessential living room accent wall color. Deep, rich, and sophisticated.

Preview any of these in your space with the [room visualizer](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/tools/room-visualizer).

## Best Nursery Paint Colors
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/best-nursery-paint-colors
Date: 2025-12-24 | Author: Paint Color HQ Staff
*Safe, soothing nursery paint colors from all major brands — including zero-VOC options, gender-neutral picks, and colors that grow with your child.*

Nurseries have unique requirements: the paint should be low- or zero-VOC for air quality, the color should be soothing for both parent and baby, and ideally the color should work as the child grows. These picks meet all three criteria.

## Zero-VOC Options

Benjamin Moore Natura, Behr Premium Plus, and Sherwin-Williams Harmony are all zero-VOC lines available in any color. Colorhouse paints are zero-VOC across their entire range.

## Gender-Neutral Picks

[**Pale Oak** (Benjamin Moore, #E8D8D0)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/pale-oak-oc-20) — A warm blush-beige that works beautifully for nurseries. Cozy without being pink.

[**Alabaster** (Sherwin-Williams, #EDEAE0)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/alabaster-7008) — A warm white that creates a bright, clean nursery that grows with the child.

## Soft Blues and Greens

[**Quiet Moments** (Benjamin Moore, #8BA7B0)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/quiet-moments-1563) — A soothing blue-green-gray. Calming and not gendered.

[**Sea Salt** (Sherwin-Williams)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/sea-salt-6204) — A soft blue-green that reads differently depending on light — always soothing.
