## Best Home Office Paint Colors for Productivity
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/best-home-office-paint-colors
Date: 2026-02-19 | Author: Paint Color HQ Staff
*Paint colors that actually boost focus and productivity — based on color psychology research, with practical picks from Sherwin-Williams, Benjamin Moore, and Behr.*

Color psychology research suggests that different hues affect cognitive performance in different ways. Blue enhances analytical thinking. Green promotes creativity. Warm neutrals reduce distraction. Here are the best home office paint colors based on your work type.

## For Analytical Work: Blues

[**Sleepy Blue** (Sherwin-Williams, #B8CAD0)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/sleepy-blue-6225) — Soft enough to not distract, blue enough to activate focus.

## For Creative Work: Greens

[**Saybrook Sage** (Benjamin Moore, #B2BAA4)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/saybrook-sage-hc-114) — Green promotes creative thinking and reduces eye strain.

## For Video Calls: Warm Neutrals

[**Accessible Beige** (Sherwin-Williams, #C4A882)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/accessible-beige-7036) — Looks professional and flattering on camera.

**Does paint finish matter in a home office?** Yes. Matte or eggshell finishes reduce screen glare and prevent eye strain during long work sessions. Avoid semi-gloss on walls.

Use the [palette generator](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/tools/palette-generator) to build a coordinated scheme for your entire office, including accent walls and trim.

---

## The Best Exterior Paint Colors to Boost Curb Appeal
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/best-exterior-paint-colors
Date: 2026-03-09 | Author: Paint Color HQ Staff
*From classic white farmhouses to moody dark siding, these exterior paint colors maximize curb appeal and complement every architectural style.*

Exterior paint color choices differ from interior selections because they interact with natural light all day, must complement landscaping and hardscape, and need to work with permanent elements like roofing and stone.

## Classic White Exteriors

[**Alabaster** (Sherwin-Williams, #EDEAE0)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/alabaster-7008) — The quintessential farmhouse white.

[**White Dove** (Benjamin Moore, #EDE6D3)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/white-dove-oc-17) — Warm enough to not look sterile in direct sunlight.

## Dark Siding Colors

Dark exteriors are trending. Navy, charcoal, and dark green siding paired with white trim creates a striking, modern farmhouse look.

[**Hale Navy** (Benjamin Moore, #3B444B)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/hale-navy-hc-154) — The most popular dark exterior color in America.

## Front Door Colors

The front door is the easiest way to add personality. Bold colors like red, yellow, and teal make a statement on an otherwise neutral exterior.

---

## Best Dining Room Paint Colors
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/best-dining-room-paint-colors
Date: 2026-02-05 | Author: Paint Color HQ Staff
*Dining room colors that set the mood for everything from weeknight dinners to formal entertaining — warm neutrals, jewel tones, and moody darks.*

Dining rooms are unique because they're primarily used in the evening under artificial light. Colors that look great in daylight might fall flat at dinnertime. The best dining room colors are rich enough to feel special and warm enough to flatter skin tones and food under warm lighting.

## Warm Neutrals for Casual Dining

[**Agreeable Gray** (Sherwin-Williams, #D1CBC1)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/agreeable-gray-7029) — Works beautifully under warm dining room lighting.

[**Edgecomb Gray** (Benjamin Moore, #D3CBBA)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/edgecomb-gray-hc-173) — Sandy and sophisticated.

## Jewel Tones for Formal Dining

[**Hale Navy** (Benjamin Moore, #3B444B)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/hale-navy-hc-154) — Creates a dramatic, elegant dining room.

Rich greens, deep plums, and moody blues all work beautifully in dining rooms because the artificial evening light warms them up. Preview any dining room color with the [room visualizer](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/tools/room-visualizer).

---

## Best Sherwin-Williams Colors for Kitchens
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/best-sherwin-williams-kitchen-colors
Date: 2025-11-27 | Author: Paint Color HQ Staff
*18 Sherwin-Williams kitchen colors — organized by style with finish recommendations, cabinet color pairings, and pricing tips.*

Sherwin-Williams dominates the kitchen paint market because of their Emerald Urethane Trim Enamel — widely considered the best paint for kitchen cabinets. Here are the 18 best SW kitchen colors.

## For White Kitchens

[**Alabaster** (Sherwin-Williams, #EDEAE0)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/alabaster-7008) — The warm white kitchen standard. Pairs with everything from marble to butcher block.

[**Pure White** (Sherwin-Williams, #EDECE6)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/pure-white-7005) — Slightly warmer than Alabaster. The #1 most specified cabinet color at Sherwin-Williams.

## For Gray Kitchens

[**Agreeable Gray** (Sherwin-Williams, #D1CBC1)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/agreeable-gray-7029) — Adds depth without closing the space in.

[**Repose Gray** (Sherwin-Williams, #C2BFB8)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/repose-gray-7015) — A true neutral gray that won't pull purple or blue.

## For Green Kitchens

Sage green kitchens are the biggest trend. SW colors like Evergreen Fog and Softened Green work beautifully with white countertops and brass hardware.

## Paint and Finish Tips

**For cabinets:** Emerald Urethane Trim Enamel in satin sheen. It self-levels, resists yellowing, and holds up to moisture and grease.

**For walls:** Duration or Emerald in eggshell sheen. Both are scrubbable and moisture-resistant.

**Wait for the sales.** Sherwin-Williams runs 30-40% off sales several times a year. Use the [paint calculator](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/tools/paint-calculator) to figure out exactly how many gallons you need.

## FAQ

**What is the most popular Sherwin-Williams color for kitchen cabinets?** Pure White (SW 7005). Alabaster (SW 7008) is a close second for those who prefer a warmer, creamier white.

**What Sherwin-Williams paint should I use on kitchen cabinets?** Emerald Urethane Trim Enamel in satin sheen.

**Can I find Benjamin Moore equivalents for these colors?** Yes. Click any [Sherwin-Williams color](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/brands/sherwin-williams) on our site to see the closest match from all 14 brands.

---

## Benjamin Moore's Most Popular White Paint Colors Ranked
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/benjamin-moore-most-popular-whites
Date: 2025-11-13 | Author: Paint Color HQ Staff
*White Dove, Chantilly Lace, Simply White, Decorator's White — ranked and compared with undertone analysis, best uses, and SW/Behr equivalents.*

Benjamin Moore makes some of the most beloved white paints in the industry, but with dozens of whites in their deck, choosing the right one is overwhelming. This guide ranks BM's most popular whites from warmest to coolest.

### 1. White Dove (OC-17)

[**White Dove** (Benjamin Moore, #EDE6D3)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/white-dove-oc-17) is the most popular white paint in America. It's a warm, creamy white with soft yellow undertones that never looks yellow on the wall. Designers use it for everything: cabinets, trim, walls, ceilings, and entire homes.

**Best for:** Cabinets, trim, whole-house color, farmhouse style. **Undertone:** Warm (soft cream). **SW equivalent:** Pure White (SW 7005).

### 2. Chantilly Lace (OC-65)

[**Chantilly Lace** (Benjamin Moore, #F5F1EB)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/chantilly-lace-2121-70) is BM's cleanest, truest white — virtually no undertone. When you want white that reads as pure white in any light, this is the one.

**Best for:** Modern interiors, trim against colored walls, ceilings, exteriors. **Undertone:** True/neutral. **SW equivalent:** High Reflective White (SW 7757).

### 3. Simply White (OC-117)

[**Simply White** (Benjamin Moore, #F1EDE3)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/simply-white-2143-70) sits between White Dove and Chantilly Lace — warmer than a true white but not as creamy as White Dove. BM's 2016 Color of the Year.

**Best for:** Kitchens, bathrooms, transitional style. **Undertone:** Slightly warm. **SW equivalent:** Extra White (SW 7006).

### 4. Decorator's White (OC-149)

[**Decorator's White** (Benjamin Moore, #EEF0EC)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/decorators-white-oc-149) is a cool white with a subtle blue-gray undertone. Fresh, modern, and slightly crisp.

**Best for:** Modern/Scandinavian style, bathrooms, rooms with cool-toned elements. **Undertone:** Cool (blue-gray). **SW equivalent:** Snowbound (SW 7004).

### 5. Pale Oak (OC-20)

[**Pale Oak** (Benjamin Moore, #E8D8D0)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/pale-oak-oc-20) is a warm off-white with a whisper of pink-beige undertone. Extremely popular for nurseries and bedrooms where you want warmth without color.

**Best for:** Bedrooms, nurseries, living rooms, whole-house neutral. **Undertone:** Warm (pink-beige).

### How to Choose Between Them

**Start with your fixed elements.** Warm countertops and wood tones -> White Dove or Pale Oak. Cool tile and gray accents -> Decorator's White or Chantilly Lace. Mixed elements -> Simply White as the universal bridge.

**Wall white vs trim white.** Many designers use one white for walls and a crisper white for trim. Classic combo: White Dove walls + Chantilly Lace trim.

Browse the full [white color family](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/family/white) and [off-white color family](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/family/off-white). Use the [compare tool](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/compare) to put any two whites side by side. Preview any white on your walls with the [room visualizer](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/tools/room-visualizer).

---

## 12 Best Behr Paint Colors for Bedrooms (2026 Picks)
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/best-behr-colors-for-bedrooms
Date: 2026-01-22 | Author: Paint Color HQ Staff
*The best Behr bedroom paint colors for 2026 — from calming blues and soft greens to warm neutrals — with real pricing, sheen advice, and CIEDE2000 cross-brand matches.*

Behr paint colors are some of the best values in the market for bedroom makeovers. Consumer Reports has ranked Behr Dynasty and Marquee at or near the top in independent blind tests, and every color below is available at Home Depot.

### Calming Blues

[**Light French Gray** (Behr, #B5C4CB)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/behr/light-french-gray-720e-2) — Don't let the name fool you. This is actually a soft blue-gray and one of Behr's all-time top sellers. LRV 53.

[**Watery** (Behr, #A8BFC8)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/behr/watery-hdc-ct-26) — A soft aqua-blue that creates instant coastal calm. LRV 50. Closest BM match: Palladian Blue (HC-144), Delta E 2.1. Browse the [blue color family](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/family/blue).

[**Adirondack Blue** (Behr, #8AAAB4)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/behr/adirondack-blue-ppu13-09) — A deeper, slate-toned blue that adds sophistication to larger bedrooms.

### Soft Greens

[**Sage Green** (Behr, #C0C5B1)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/behr/sage-green-ppu11-07) — A true sage that pairs with natural wood furniture and linen textiles.

[**Botanical Green** (Behr, #B8BFA8)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/behr/botanical-green-ppu11-03) — A slightly warmer, dustier sage that reads almost neutral. Delta E to SW Evergreen Fog is under 3.0. See the [green color family](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/family/green).

### Warm Neutrals

[**Silver Drop** (Behr, #D2CBBD)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/behr/silver-drop-790c-2) — Behr's answer to Agreeable Gray. LRV 59.

[**Dolphin Fin** (Behr, #D8CFC0)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/behr/dolphin-fin-790c-3) — A slightly cooler greige that works with both warm and cool elements. Compare with Silver Drop using our [color comparison tool](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/compare).

### Soft Whites

[**Cameo White** (Behr, #F0E8D8)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/behr/cameo-white-mq3-32) — A warm white with subtle cream undertones. The Behr equivalent of Benjamin Moore White Dove. LRV 81.

[**Ultra Pure White** (Behr, #F2ECE0)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/behr/ultra-pure-white-50) — Behr's cleanest, crispest white with virtually no undertone.

### Which Behr Paint Line for Bedrooms?

**Behr Dynasty ($45-55/gal):** Premium. One-coat coverage, scuff-proof. Best for primary bedrooms and kids' rooms.
**Behr Marquee ($40-50/gal):** One-coat hide guaranteed. Great for guest rooms.
**Behr Ultra ($32-38/gal):** Value pick. Two coats on most colors. Fine for spare bedrooms.
**Behr Premium Plus ($28-32/gal):** Budget tier. Adequate for ceilings and closets.

Use the [paint calculator](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/tools/paint-calculator) to estimate gallons needed.

### Why Behr Over Premium Brands?

**Price savings are real.** Behr Dynasty runs $45-55/gallon while BM Aura costs $80-90 and SW Emerald runs $75-85 at full price. For a bedroom needing 2 gallons, switching to Behr saves $50-80.

**Convenience matters for DIYers.** Home Depot stocks every Behr color in-store and is open evenings and weekends.

Read the full [three-brand comparison](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/behr-vs-sherwin-williams-vs-benjamin-moore) for details.

---

## Paint Color Trends 2026: What Designers Are Predicting
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/paint-color-trends-2026
Date: 2026-03-13 | Author: Paint Color HQ Staff
*From rich terracotta to earthy olive green, here are the paint color trends that will define 2026 — based on early brand announcements, designer surveys, and search data.*

The warm-toned revolution that began in 2024 is accelerating. After a decade of cool grays, homeowners are embracing richer, more expressive colors.

## Trend 1: Earthy Greens Dominate

The biggest trend of 2026 is confirmed by the Color of the Year picks: earthy, muted greens are everywhere. Behr's [**Hidden Gem** (Behr, #596D69)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/behr/hidden-gem-n430-6a) is a smoky jade, while Valspar's [**Warm Eucalyptus** (Valspar, #7C7F70)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/valspar/warm-eucalyptus-8004-28f) is a grounded gray-green. Two of five major brands chose green — that's not a coincidence. Explore the [green color family](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/family/green).

## Trend 2: Warm Neutrals Get Richer

Sherwin-Williams' [**Universal Khaki** (Sherwin-Williams, #b8a992)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/universal-khaki-6150) captures the new neutral — warm, sandy, and golden rather than the cool grays of the 2010s. Benjamin Moore's [**Silhouette** (Benjamin Moore, #58514d)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/silhouette-af-655) shows that even dark neutrals are getting warmer.

## Trend 3: Warm Plum & Berry

Purple is making a comeback — not bright violet, but warm, muted plums and berry tones. See the [purple family](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/family/purple) for options.

## Trend 4: Butter Yellow Returns

Soft, buttery yellows are appearing in kitchens, breakfast nooks, and entryways. Browse the [yellow family](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/family/yellow).

## Trend 5: The Death of "Safe Neutral"

Homeowners are moving away from builder-grade greige and choosing colors that reflect their personality. Even neutrals are getting more interesting: warm mushroom tones, sandy taupes, and rosy beiges.

## What's Fading Out

**Cool gray:** The 2015-2020 darling continues to decline. **All-white everything:** Giving way to warm whites paired with color. **Navy overload:** Navy remains popular but is plateauing.

Use the [room visualizer](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/tools/room-visualizer) to preview any trending color on your walls.

---

## Sherwin-Williams vs Benjamin Moore: The Complete Comparison
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/sherwin-williams-vs-benjamin-moore
Date: 2025-11-27 | Author: Paint Color HQ Staff
*A detailed head-to-head comparison of America's two most popular premium paint brands — covering quality, price, color matching, availability, and which to choose.*

Sherwin-Williams and Benjamin Moore are the two most specified paint brands among designers and contractors. Both make excellent paint, but they differ in meaningful ways.

## Price: Advantage SW (With Sales)

**Benjamin Moore Regal Select:** $65-$75/gal. **Aura:** $80-$90/gal. **Sherwin-Williams SuperPaint:** $60-$70/gal. **Emerald:** $75-$85/gal. But SW runs 30-40% off sales 4-6 times per year, dropping Emerald to $50-$60. BM rarely discounts.

## Popular Colors Head-to-Head

[**Agreeable Gray** (Sherwin-Williams, #D1CBC1)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/agreeable-gray-7029) vs [**Revere Pewter** (Benjamin Moore, #CCC7B9)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/revere-pewter-hc-172) — America's two most popular grays. Delta E: 2.4.

[**Pure White** (Sherwin-Williams, #F3EEE0)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/pure-white-7005) vs [**White Dove** (Benjamin Moore, #EDE6D3)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/white-dove-oc-17) — Both warm whites, but White Dove is slightly creamier.

[**Alabaster** (Sherwin-Williams, #EDEAE0)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/alabaster-7008) vs [**Simply White** (Benjamin Moore, #F1ECE0)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/simply-white-2143-70) — Nearly identical warm whites.

## Quality: Advantage BM (Slightly)

BM Aura has a slight edge in color depth and true one-coat coverage. SW Emerald excels in self-leveling and workability. For cabinets and trim, many pros prefer BM Advance (a waterborne alkyd).

## Color Selection

Benjamin Moore: ~3,500 colors (largest palette). Sherwin-Williams: ~1,700 colors (more curated).

## Availability

SW wins: 4,000+ company-owned stores plus Lowe's, open weekends. BM: independent dealers only, limited hours.

## Who Should Choose SW

You value convenience, want to wait for sales, your contractor already has an SW account.

## Who Should Choose BM

You want the richest color depth, you're painting cabinets, your designer specced BM colors.

## The Best of Both Worlds

Many homeowners use both: BM Advance for cabinets and trim, SW Emerald for walls. Use the [compare tool](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/compare) to make sure your BM trim white matches your SW wall color.

---

## Paint Sheen Guide: Flat vs Eggshell vs Satin vs Semi-Gloss
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/paint-sheen-guide
Date: 2025-10-16 | Author: Paint Color HQ Staff
*Choosing the right sheen is just as important as choosing the right color. This guide explains every finish level and which rooms need which sheen.*

## Flat / Matte

**Sheen level:** 0-5% gloss. Completely non-reflective. Hides surface imperfections. **Pros:** Best for hiding wall imperfections. Richest, deepest color appearance. No glare. **Cons:** Difficult to clean. Not moisture resistant. Shows touch-ups easily. **Best for:** Ceilings, adult bedrooms, formal dining rooms, low-traffic rooms.

## Eggshell

**Sheen level:** 10-25% gloss. A soft, velvety finish with just a hint of luster. **Pros:** Easy to wipe clean. More durable than flat. Still hides minor imperfections. The most popular residential wall finish. **Cons:** Shows touch-ups slightly more than flat. **Best for:** Living rooms, bedrooms, hallways, dining rooms, home offices — basically most walls in your home.

## Satin

**Sheen level:** 25-35% gloss. A noticeable soft sheen. **Pros:** Very easy to clean. More moisture resistant. Durable in high-traffic areas. **Cons:** Shows wall imperfections more than eggshell. **Best for:** Kitchens, bathrooms, kids' rooms, laundry rooms, high-traffic hallways.

## Semi-Gloss

**Sheen level:** 35-70% gloss. Noticeably shiny, reflective. **Pros:** Extremely durable and easy to clean. Highly moisture resistant. Creates visual definition between trim and walls. **Cons:** Shows every imperfection. Requires careful surface prep. **Best for:** Trim, baseboards, door frames, cabinets, bathroom walls above tile.

## High-Gloss

**Sheen level:** 70%+ gloss. Mirror-like. **Pros:** Most durable and cleanable finish. **Cons:** Shows every imperfection dramatically. **Best for:** Front doors, accent furniture, built-in shelving.

## The Simple Room-by-Room Rule

**Ceilings:** Flat. **Most walls:** Eggshell. **Kitchens & bathrooms:** Satin. **Trim & cabinets:** Semi-gloss. **Front door:** Semi-gloss or high-gloss.

---

## How to Test Paint Samples the Right Way
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/how-to-test-paint-samples
Date: 2025-10-23 | Author: Paint Color HQ Staff
*Stop making color decisions from tiny paint chips. Learn the right way to test paint samples so you choose a color you'll love for years.*

A 1-inch paint chip cannot tell you what a color will look like on your walls. Colors shift dramatically based on the size of the surface, your room's lighting, adjacent colors, and the time of day.

## Why Paint Chips Lie

When you look at a tiny paint chip, your brain interprets the color differently than when that same color covers an entire wall. Small samples appear darker and more saturated. A medium gray on a chip can look like a light gray on a wall. A subtle blue undertone that's invisible on a chip becomes unmistakable at room scale.

## Option 1: Peel-and-Stick Samples

Both [Sherwin-Williams](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/brands/sherwin-williams) and Benjamin Moore offer large peel-and-stick color samples (~8x8 inches). At $5-8 each, they're the cheapest way to test. Move them around the room, hold them next to trim and countertops. **Tip:** Don't press them flat against the wall. Hold the sample about an inch away.

## Option 2: Paint Sample Boards

Buy a quart sample ($8-15) and paint a large piece of white poster board — at least 2 feet square. Apply two coats and let it dry fully. This gives you a portable, true-color sample.

## Option 3: Paint Directly on the Wall

Paint at least a 2x2 foot square. Apply two coats. Paint samples on at least two different walls — one with direct light, one without.

## The 24-Hour Rule

**Never judge a paint sample immediately.** Wet paint looks different from dry paint. Colors shift with different light throughout the day. **Morning light** is warm and golden. **Midday light** is most neutral. **Afternoon light** is warm and orange. **Evening** (artificial light) is where most bad color decisions reveal themselves.

## How Many Colors to Sample

**Three is the sweet spot.** Use our [color search](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/search) to narrow down, then sample your top three. Use the [room visualizer](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/tools/room-visualizer) for a quick digital preview before buying physical samples.

---

## Color Theory for Home Decorators: A Practical Guide
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/color-theory-for-home-decorators
Date: 2025-09-25 | Author: Paint Color HQ Staff
*Skip the art school jargon. This practical color theory guide teaches you how to use the color wheel, build palettes, and combine colors like a designer.*

You don't need an art degree to understand color — you just need a few practical principles.

## The Color Wheel (Simplified)

The color wheel arranges colors by relationship: **primary** colors (red, blue, yellow) combine to make **secondary** colors (orange, green, purple). What matters for decorating is the relationships between colors on the wheel.

## Complementary Colors: Bold Contrast

Colors directly opposite each other on the wheel — blue and orange, red and green, yellow and purple. In home decor, this works best when one color dominates and the other accents.

**Example:** [**Naval** (Sherwin-Williams, #34405A)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/naval-6244) blue walls with warm orange-brown leather furniture. Dynamic without being chaotic.

## Analogous Colors: Easy Harmony

Colors next to each other on the wheel — blue, blue-green, and green. These combinations are inherently harmonious and hard to mess up.

**Example:** [**Saybrook Sage** (Benjamin Moore, #B2BAA4)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/saybrook-sage-hc-114) green walls, [**Quiet Moments** (Benjamin Moore, #8BA7B0)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/quiet-moments-1563) blue-green bathroom, [**Silver Mist** (Benjamin Moore, #A5B8C4)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/silver-mist-1619) blue bedroom. All adjacent on the wheel. All harmonious.

## The 60-30-10 Rule

**60%** of the room should be one dominant color (usually walls), **30%** should be a secondary color (furniture, rugs), and **10%** should be an accent (throw pillows, art). Use the [palette generator](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/tools/palette-generator) to build a 3-color scheme.

## Warm vs Cool: The Temperature Rule

**Connected spaces should share the same temperature.** A warm living room flowing into a cool kitchen feels disjointed. A warm living room flowing into a warm kitchen feels intentional. See our [warm vs cool guide](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/warm-vs-cool-paint-colors).

## Saturation and Value

*Designer rooms almost always use low-saturation colors.* Bright, vivid colors feel juvenile or dated. Muted, grayed-out versions of the same hue feel sophisticated and timeless. When in doubt, go more muted.

## Putting It Together

**Step 1:** Identify your fixed elements and note whether they're warm or cool.
**Step 2:** Choose a wall color in the same temperature family, at low saturation.
**Step 3:** Apply 60-30-10.
**Step 4:** Sample before committing. The [room visualizer](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/tools/room-visualizer) gives a digital preview; physical samples give the final answer.

---

## The Best Paint Colors for North-Facing Rooms
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/best-paint-colors-north-facing-rooms
Date: 2025-12-18 | Author: Paint Color HQ Staff
*North-facing rooms get cool, indirect light all day. These warm-toned, high-LRV colors counteract the gray cast and make the space feel bright and inviting.*

North-facing rooms receive no direct sunlight — only cool, diffused light that gives everything a slightly blue-gray cast. The fix is simple: choose colors with warm undertones and a high LRV (Light Reflectance Value) so they bounce back as much light as possible.

## Why LRV Matters in North-Facing Rooms

LRV measures how much light a color reflects on a scale of 0 (pure black) to 100 (pure white). In north-facing rooms, aim for LRV 55 or higher.

## Warm Whites & Creams

[**White Dove** (Benjamin Moore, #EDE6D3)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/white-dove-oc-17) — The go-to warm white for north-facing rooms. LRV 85.

[**Pure White** (Sherwin-Williams, #F3EEE0)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/pure-white-7005) — Just enough cream to prevent the cold, clinical look. LRV 84.

[**Alabaster** (Sherwin-Williams, #F0EBE0)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/alabaster-7008) — A soft, buttery white. LRV 82.

## Warm Beiges & Greiges

[**Edgecomb Gray** (Benjamin Moore, #D3CBBA)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/edgecomb-gray-hc-173) — Warm greige with sandy undertones. LRV 63.

[**Accessible Beige** (Sherwin-Williams, #C4A882)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/accessible-beige-7036) — True warm beige that holds its warmth even in cool light. LRV 58.

[**Shoji White** (Sherwin-Williams, #D5C8B5)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/shoji-white-7042) — Splits the difference between white and beige.

## Soft Yellows & Golds

[**Hawthorne Yellow** (Benjamin Moore, #E8DCC8)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/hawthorne-yellow-hc-4) — A muted, historical gold. LRV 67.

## Warm Pinks & Blush

[**Pale Oak** (Benjamin Moore, #E8D8D0)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/pale-oak-oc-20) — Reads as warm blush-beige in north light. LRV 70.

## Colors to Avoid

**Cool grays** (Repose Gray, Stonington Gray) will look even colder. **Pure whites** (Chantilly Lace) will feel stark. **Cool blues and greens** amplify the existing blue cast. Use the [compare tool](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/compare) to see warm vs cool grays side by side.

---

## The Best Paint Colors for East-Facing Rooms
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/best-paint-colors-east-facing-rooms
Date: 2025-12-26 | Author: Paint Color HQ Staff
*East-facing rooms get warm morning sun and cool afternoon shade. These colors look great in both conditions.*

East-facing rooms are the trickiest to paint because the light changes dramatically throughout the day. Morning brings warm, golden sunlight. By afternoon, that warmth disappears and the room shifts to cool, shadowy light.

## The East-Facing Challenge

A color that looks perfect at 9 AM can look completely different at 3 PM. The sweet spot is colors with balanced or slightly warm undertones and moderate-to-high LRV (50-75).

## Balanced Neutrals

[**Balboa Mist** (Benjamin Moore, #C8BFB0)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/balboa-mist-oc-27) — Warm gray-beige that reads cozy in morning light and sophisticated gray in afternoon shade. LRV 67.

[**Agreeable Gray** (Sherwin-Williams, #D2CBBD)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/agreeable-gray-7029) — Its balanced warm-cool undertone makes it a chameleon for any light condition. LRV 60.

[**Edgecomb Gray** (Benjamin Moore, #D3CBBA)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/edgecomb-gray-hc-173) — Slightly sandy. Catches morning sun beautifully without going cold in afternoon.

## Soft Greens

[**Saybrook Sage** (Benjamin Moore, #B2BAA4)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/saybrook-sage-hc-114) — Glows golden in morning light and settles into serene green by afternoon.

[**Softened Green** (Sherwin-Williams, #C2C5B4)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/softened-green-6177) — Barely green, almost gray. LRV 55.

## Warm Whites

[**White Dove** (Benjamin Moore, #EDE6D3)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/white-dove-oc-17) — Creamy and luminous all day. LRV 85.

[**Alabaster** (Sherwin-Williams, #F0EBE0)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/alabaster-7008) — Buttery white that holds warmth even as afternoon shade rolls in.

## Soft Blues (With Caution)

[**Quiet Moments** (Benjamin Moore, #8BA7B0)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/quiet-moments-1563) — Blue-green-gray that shifts beautifully — blue in morning, gray-green in afternoon.

[**Sleepy Blue** (Sherwin-Williams, #B8CAD0)](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/sleepy-blue-6225) — Dusty powder blue with enough gray to prevent going cold.

## Colors to Avoid

**Bright yellows** — morning sun will make them overwhelming. **Cool grays without warm undertones** — fine in morning, depressing by afternoon. **Very dark colors** below LRV 30.

**Test at both extremes.** Paint your sample and check at 9 AM (peak warmth) and 3 PM (peak cool). Use the [room visualizer](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/tools/room-visualizer) for a quick preview.

---

# Brand Descriptions

## Sherwin-Williams
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/brands/sherwin-williams

Sherwin-Williams is America's largest paint company, with over 4,000 retail locations and a palette of 1,700+ colors. Founded in 1866 in Cleveland, Ohio, Sherwin-Williams has grown into a global coatings leader trusted by homeowners, contractors, and architects alike. Their most popular color — [Agreeable Gray SW 7029](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/agreeable-gray-sw-7029) — has been the best-selling paint color in the country for five consecutive years. The brand is known for excellent contractor-grade formulas and frequent 30-40% off sales.

Other top Sherwin-Williams colors include [Pure White SW 7005](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/pure-white-sw-7005), [Alabaster SW 7008](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/alabaster-sw-7008), [Repose Gray SW 7015](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/repose-gray-sw-7015), and [Naval SW 6244](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/sherwin-williams/naval-sw-6244).

Top product lines include **Emerald** (premium, self-leveling), **Duration** (extreme durability for exteriors and high-traffic interiors), **SuperPaint** (solid mid-range), and **Cashmere** (smooth, buttery application). Price range: $55-$80+ per gallon depending on the line. Pro tip: sign up for their PaintPerks program to get notified of sales events.

---

## Benjamin Moore
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/brands/benjamin-moore

Benjamin Moore is the premium paint brand preferred by interior designers and professional painters. Founded in 1883 in Brooklyn, New York, Benjamin Moore has built a reputation for rich, true-to-swatch color over more than 140 years. Their proprietary Gennex color system produces exceptionally accurate, consistent color with every can, and their 3,500+ color palette is the largest among major paint brands. Benjamin Moore is sold exclusively through independent paint stores and hardware stores — you won't find it at Home Depot or Lowe's.

The most popular Benjamin Moore colors consistently include [White Dove OC-17](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/white-dove-oc-17), [Edgecomb Gray HC-173](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/edgecomb-gray-hc-173), [Revere Pewter HC-172](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/revere-pewter-hc-172), [Chantilly Lace OC-65](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/chantilly-lace-oc-65), [Hale Navy HC-154](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/hale-navy-hc-154), and [Simply White OC-117](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/benjamin-moore/simply-white-oc-117).

Top product lines include **Aura** (the premium flagship — true one-coat coverage with Color Lock technology), **Regal Select** (excellent all-around performance), **Ben** (budget-friendly entry point), and **Natura** (zero-VOC for nurseries and bedrooms). Price range: $60-$85+ per gallon.

---

## Behr
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/brands/behr

Behr is the most accessible major paint brand in the United States, sold exclusively at Home Depot with a palette of 3,000+ colors. As the go-to paint for DIY homeowners, Behr has earned top marks from Consumer Reports, which has consistently ranked Behr Marquee at or near the top in blind coverage and durability tests. Behr's thick consistency and one-coat formulas are especially forgiving for first-time painters.

The most popular Behr paint colors include [Silver Drop 790C-2](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/behr/silver-drop-790c-2), [Ultra Pure White 1850](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/behr/ultra-pure-white-1850), [Cameo White W-D-200](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/behr/cameo-white-w-d-200), [Dolphin Fin 790C-3](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/behr/dolphin-fin-790c-3), and [Whisper White W-D-300](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/behr/whisper-white-w-d-300).

Top product lines include **Dynasty** ($45-55/gal, premium, one-coat, scuff-resistant), **Marquee** ($40-50/gal, excellent coverage), **Ultra** ($32-38/gal, solid mid-range), and **Premium Plus** ($28-32/gal, budget-friendly). Behr is the most affordable of the big three brands.

---

## PPG
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/brands/ppg

PPG is one of the world's largest paint and coatings manufacturers, with roots going back to 1883 when Pittsburgh Plate Glass Company was founded. PPG paints are available at Home Depot (PPG Timeless and PPG Diamond labels), PPG Paints stores, and independent dealers.

Popular PPG colors include [Delicate White 1001-1](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/ppg/delicate-white-1001-1), [Gypsum 1006-1](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/ppg/gypsum-1006-1), [Swirling Smoke 1007-2](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/ppg/swirling-smoke-1007-2), and [Stonehenge Greige 1024-5](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/ppg/stonehenge-greige-1024-5).

Top product lines include **PPG Timeless** (premium, one-coat guarantee), **PPG Diamond** (durable mid-range), and classic **Pittsburgh Paints**. PPG also owns **Glidden** and **Olympic**. Price range: $35-$70/gallon.

---

## Valspar
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/brands/valspar

Valspar is one of the most popular paint brands in America, available exclusively at Lowe's stores nationwide. Now owned by Sherwin-Williams (acquired in 2017), Valspar benefits from the parent company's color science while maintaining its own distinct palette of 2,000+ colors and competitive pricing.

Popular Valspar colors include [Warm Eucalyptus 8004-28F](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/valspar/warm-eucalyptus-8004-28f) (their 2026 Color of the Year), [Safari Beige 6006-2B](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/valspar/safari-beige-6006-2b), and [Gray Silt 6002-1C](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/valspar/gray-silt-6002-1c).

Top product lines include **Valspar Signature** (premium one-coat), **Valspar Reserve** (ultra-premium), and **Valspar Ultra** (mid-range). Price range: $30-$55/gallon.

---

## Dunn-Edwards
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/brands/dunn-edwards

Dunn-Edwards is a premium West Coast paint brand founded in 1925 in Los Angeles, California. With over 150 company-owned stores across California, Arizona, Nevada, New Mexico, and Texas, Dunn-Edwards is known for zero-VOC and low-VOC formulas. Their SUPREMA line earned GreenWise certification.

Popular colors include [Swiss Coffee DEW341](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/dunn-edwards/swiss-coffee-dew341), [Bone China DEW339](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/dunn-edwards/bone-china-dew339), and [Foggy Day DE6226](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/dunn-edwards/foggy-day-de6226).

Top product lines include **SUPREMA** (premium interior), **Evershield** (exterior), and **Aristoshield** (stain and scuff resistant). Price range: $45-$65/gallon.

---

## Farrow & Ball
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/brands/farrow-ball

Farrow & Ball is a British luxury paint brand founded in 1946 in Dorset, England, renowned for deeply pigmented, richly textured finishes. Each color is handcrafted using exceptionally high concentrations of pigment that create extraordinary depth and complexity — especially visible as natural light changes throughout the day. The brand has a cult following among interior designers and architects.

The most iconic Farrow & Ball colors include [Hague Blue No.30](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/farrow-ball/hague-blue-30), [Elephant's Breath No.229](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/farrow-ball/elephants-breath-229), [Cornforth White No.228](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/farrow-ball/cornforth-white-228), [Ammonite No.274](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/farrow-ball/ammonite-274), and [Stiffkey Blue No.281](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/colors/farrow-ball/stiffkey-blue-281).

Their palette is deliberately curated at approximately 150 colors. Finish names: **Modern Emulsion** (durable wipeable matte), **Estate Emulsion** (chalky ultra-matte), **Full Gloss** (for woodwork), and **Modern Eggshell** (mid-sheen for kitchens and bathrooms). Price range: $100-$120/gallon — the most expensive major brand.

---

## Kilz
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/brands/kilz

Kilz is best known as America's #1 primer brand, but they also offer a full line of interior paints. Founded in 1954 and now a sister company to Behr under the Masco Corporation. Products sold at Home Depot and Walmart. Flagship paint line: **Kilz Tribute** — premium interior paint + primer. Price range: $30-$40/gallon.

---

## Dutch Boy
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/brands/dutch-boy

Dutch Boy is a heritage paint brand founded in 1907, now a subsidiary of Sherwin-Williams. Known for its Twist & Pour paint can innovation. Primarily sold at Menards stores. Product lines include **Platinum Plus**, **Dura Clean**, and **Forever**. Price range: $25-$45/gallon.

---

## Vista Paint
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/brands/vista-paint

Vista Paint is a family-owned paint manufacturer founded in 1956 in Southern California. Operates 50+ company-owned retail stores across California and Southern Nevada. Manufacturing on 100% solar energy. Product lines include **V-PRO 5000**, **Scuff-Tec**, and **Carefree**. Price range: $25-$50/gallon.

---

## Hirshfield's
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/brands/hirshfields

Hirshfield's is a fourth-generation, family-owned paint and decorating company based in Minneapolis, Minnesota, with over 130 years of history. Largest manufacturer of trade sales paint in Minnesota, producing over 5,000 gallons daily. Operates 30+ decorating centers across Minnesota, North Dakota, South Dakota, and Wisconsin. Product lines include **Platinum Ceramic**, **Reserve**, and **Wash & Wear**. Price range: $47-$69/gallon.

---

## Colorhouse
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/brands/colorhouse

Colorhouse is a woman-owned, eco-friendly paint company founded in 2005 in Portland, Oregon. Zero-VOC, low-odor, free of reproductive toxins and chemical solvents. Excellent for nurseries, bedrooms, and homes with children or pets. Artist-crafted palette organized into thematic families: Air, Stone, Clay, Leaf, Water, and Petal. Price range: $36-$50/gallon.

---

## RAL
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/brands/ral

RAL is the European standard color matching system, originating in Germany in 1927. RAL defines standardized color codes used globally across industries — architecture, automotive, industrial coatings, powder coating, and manufacturing. The **RAL Classic** collection contains 216 colors. Also includes **RAL Design System Plus** (1,825 colors) and **RAL Effect** (490 colors including metallics).

---

## MPC (Master Paint Colors)
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/brands/mpc

MPC is a paint color system offering a wide selection of architectural colors. Commonly used as reference standards in the coatings industry for color matching and specification.

---

# Tool Descriptions

## Room Color Visualizer
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/tools/room-visualizer

The Paint Color HQ Room Visualizer lets you preview paint colors on every surface in a room before you commit to a single can of paint. Click on any region — walls, ceiling, trim, accent wall, or floor — to select it, then choose a color from the picker or type in a specific hex code. The room updates instantly so you can experiment freely without any waiting.

Once you find a color you love, use the "Find Paint Match" feature to see which real-world paints from 14 major brands — including Sherwin-Williams, Benjamin Moore, Behr, Valspar, PPG, Dunn-Edwards, and Farrow & Ball — are the closest match. Every suggestion links to its full color detail page so you can explore undertones, coordinating colors, and cross-brand alternatives.

### How to Use

1. **Select a region** by clicking directly on the room image or using the buttons below it.
2. **Pick a color** using the color picker or type a hex code. The room updates instantly.
3. **Find paint matches** to discover which real paint colors from major brands are closest to your selection.

Screen colors differ from physical paint. Always verify with a swatch or sample before purchasing.

### Why Preview Before You Paint

Paint always looks different on a full wall than it does on a tiny swatch card at the store. Lighting conditions, room dimensions, and the colors surrounding a surface all change how a shade is perceived. A warm beige can read pink under cool fluorescent light, and a soft gray can shift blue next to certain wood tones.

Testing digitally saves real money. Instead of buying three or four sample pots at $5 to $8 each, you can narrow your choices down to a single front-runner in minutes.

### Tips

- **Start with the wall color.** Walls cover the most surface area, so they set the overall tone.
- **Use contrasting trim.** White or off-white trim against colored walls is a timeless combination.
- **Experiment with the accent wall.** A single bold wall can anchor a space without overwhelming it.
- **Convert custom colors to real paint.** Any color you pick can be matched to a real product using the "Find Paint Match" button.

---

## Photo Color Identifier
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/tools/color-identifier

The Photo Color Identifier lets you extract real paint color matches from any image on your device. Upload a photo of a room, a piece of furniture, a fabric swatch, or anything else that caught your eye — then click on the exact spot you want to match. The tool reads the precise pixel color at that point and compares it against our database of over 25,000 paint colors from 14 major brands.

Matching is done using the Delta E 2000 color difference formula, which measures how close two colors are the way the human eye actually perceives them — not just by raw RGB values.

### How It Works

1. **Upload a photo** from your device or snap one with your camera. Any image format works.
2. **Click a spot** on the image to sample the color. The tool reads the exact pixel color at that point.
3. **Get matches** instantly from 25,000+ paint colors using Delta E 2000 for perceptually accurate matching.

### Best Photos for Accurate Color Matching

- **Use natural light.** Colors shift dramatically under artificial lighting. Avoid flash.
- **Avoid shadows and glare.** Angle your camera to eliminate glare on shiny surfaces.
- **Shoot straight-on at the actual surface.** Not a photo of a screen showing the color.
- **Click the most representative area.** Avoid edges or areas where two colors meet.

### What to Do After You Find Your Color

Once identified, preview it with the [Room Visualizer](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/tools/room-visualizer). Use the [Color Comparison tool](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/compare) to see matches from multiple brands side by side. Read our [undertones guide](https://www.paintcolorhq.com/blog/understanding-paint-color-undertones) to understand how undertones shift with lighting. Always order a physical sample before buying full gallons.

---

## Palette Generator
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/tools/palette-generator

The Palette Generator builds coordinated color schemes from any starting color you choose. Pick a color using the color picker or type in a hex code, and the tool instantly generates five distinct palettes based on proven color harmony principles — complementary, analogous, triadic, split-complementary, and monochromatic. Each palette assigns colors to specific room roles: Walls, Trim, Accent, and Pop.

Every color in every palette is matched to a real paint color you can actually buy from our database of over 25,000 colors across 14 major brands.

### How It Works

1. **Pick a starting color** — use the color picker or type a hex code. Optionally filter results to a single brand.
2. **Choose a scheme** — five coordinated palettes appear instantly, each with Walls, Trim, Accent, and Pop roles.
3. **Save or visualize** — open any palette in the Room Visualizer to preview it on walls.

### Color Harmony Types Explained

- **Complementary** — Colors from opposite sides of the color wheel (blue and orange). High contrast, bold accent walls.
- **Analogous** — Colors next to each other on the wheel (blue, blue-green, green). Harmonious and calm, ideal for bedrooms and living rooms.
- **Triadic** — Three colors evenly spaced around the wheel. Balanced but vibrant. Good for playrooms and creative spaces.
- **Split-complementary** — Base color plus the two colors adjacent to its complement. Contrast with nuance.
- **Monochromatic** — Different shades of a single hue. Safest approach for sophisticated, layered look. Effective in small spaces.

### Building a Room Palette

A well-designed room palette follows the Walls + Trim + Accent + Pop structure. The wall color covers the most surface area and sets the mood. Trim should contrast enough to define architectural details. The accent goes on a feature wall or large furniture piece. The pop color is used sparingly — throw pillows, art, statement pieces.

---

## Paint Calculator
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/tools/paint-calculator

The paint calculator takes the guesswork out of buying the right amount of paint. Enter room dimensions — length, width, and ceiling height — to calculate total wall area. Subtract areas you won't paint by entering doors and windows. The calculator deducts approximately 21 square feet per standard door and 15 square feet per average window.

### How We Calculate

Coverage rate: **350 square feet per gallon** (industry standard for interior latex on smooth surface).

1. **Wall area** = 2 x (length + width) x height
2. **Subtract openings**: each standard door ~21 sq ft, each window ~15 sq ft
3. **Multiply by coats**: most jobs need 2 coats
4. **Divide by 350** sq ft/gallon and round up

### How Much Paint Do I Need?

One gallon covers roughly 350-400 sq ft on a smooth, primed surface in a single coat. For a typical 12x12 ft bedroom with 8-ft ceilings, that's about 384 sq ft of wall space — one gallon for the first coat, a second gallon for coat two.

Most projects require two coats. Dark-to-light changes may need a tinted primer plus two topcoats. Ceilings typically need just one gallon for an average room. For trim, a quart covers 1-2 doors or 50-75 linear feet of baseboard.

### Tips for Accurate Estimates

- **Measure twice.** Small errors compound across four walls.
- **Account for wall texture.** Knockdown, orange peel, or brick absorbs 15-20% more paint.
- **Buy 10% extra for touch-ups.** Same-batch paint ensures perfect color match.
- **Primer needs separate calculation.** Primer covers 200-300 sq ft/gallon.

---

# Frequently Asked Questions
URL: https://www.paintcolorhq.com/faq

### How do I match a paint color from one brand to another?
Search for any color on Paint Color HQ and we'll show you the closest matches from every other brand in our database. We use CIEDE2000, the industry-standard formula for measuring perceptual color difference, to compute how close each match is. Every result includes a Delta E score — below 1.0 means the difference is invisible to most people, and below 3.0 means it's very close. You can also use our Color Search to find a specific color by name, number, or hex code.

### What is Delta E and what do the numbers mean?
Delta E is a number that represents the perceptual difference between two colors. A Delta E of 0 means the colors are identical. Below 1.0, most people can't see any difference. Between 1.0 and 3.0, a trained eye might notice. Above 3.0, the difference is clearly visible. Above 5.0, the colors are noticeably different. Paint Color HQ uses the CIEDE2000 formula, which is the most accurate version of Delta E for how humans actually perceive color differences.

### What paint undertone should I choose?
Undertone is the subtle base color beneath a paint's surface color. Whites can lean warm (yellow, cream) or cool (blue, gray). Grays can lean blue, green, or purple. Choosing the right undertone depends on your room's lighting, flooring, and fixed elements like countertops or cabinets. A warm-toned room (wood floors, warm lighting) usually looks best with warm or neutral undertones. A cool-toned room (gray tile, north-facing light) pairs well with cool undertones. On Paint Color HQ, every color is tagged warm, cool, or neutral so you can filter by undertone.

### How much paint do I need for a room?
A gallon of paint typically covers about 350-400 square feet with one coat. For a standard 12x12 room with 8-foot ceilings, you'd need about 1.5 gallons for two coats on the walls. Our Paint Calculator lets you enter your exact room dimensions, number of doors and windows, and desired number of coats to get a precise estimate.

### Can I match Sherwin-Williams colors to Benjamin Moore?
Yes. Paint Color HQ has the complete color libraries for both Sherwin-Williams and Benjamin Moore (plus 12 other brands). Search for any Sherwin-Williams color and you'll see the closest Benjamin Moore equivalents ranked by Delta E accuracy — and vice versa.

### What's the difference between paint sheen levels?
Paint sheen refers to how glossy or matte the finish is. From least to most shiny: Flat/Matte (no shine, hides imperfections, good for ceilings). Eggshell (slight sheen, most popular for living rooms and bedrooms). Satin (noticeable sheen, cleans easily, good for kitchens and bathrooms). Semi-Gloss (shiny, very durable, ideal for trim and cabinets). High-Gloss (shiniest, used for accent pieces and doors).

### How do I identify a paint color from a photo?
Use our Photo Color Identifier tool. Upload any photo, click any spot, and we'll find the closest matching paint colors from all 14 brands. The tool extracts the exact color value at that pixel and runs it through our CIEDE2000 matching algorithm.

### Are the colors on screen accurate to real paint?
Digital colors are always approximations. Every screen displays colors slightly differently, and real paint looks different depending on the finish, number of coats, primer, and lighting. We always recommend ordering physical paint samples before committing to a color.

### What paint brands does Paint Color HQ cover?
We cover 14 brands: Sherwin-Williams, Benjamin Moore, Behr, PPG, Valspar, Dunn-Edwards, Farrow & Ball, Colorhouse, Hirshfield's, Kilz, Dutch Boy, Vista Paint, RAL, and MPC. Over 25,000 individual colors total.

### How do I choose a paint color for my room?
Start by looking at what's already in the room that you can't change — flooring, countertops, large furniture. Pick up on the undertones in those fixed elements and choose paint with a complementary undertone. Consider natural light: north-facing rooms benefit from warmer colors, south-facing rooms can handle cooler tones. Use our Room Visualizer to preview colors on walls, and our Palette Generator to build a coordinated scheme.

### What is the most popular paint color?
Neutral tones dominate: warm whites, light grays, and greige shades are consistently the best sellers. Sherwin-Williams Agreeable Gray, Benjamin Moore White Dove, and Behr Silver Drop are perennial favorites. For 2025-2026, earthy tones like warm terracotta, sage green, and muted blues have been trending.

### Is Paint Color HQ free to use?
Yes. All tools — color search, cross-brand matching, room visualizer, photo color identifier, palette generator, and paint calculator — are completely free with no account required.

---

# Key Color Science Reference

## What is CIEDE2000?

CIEDE2000 (also written CIE DE 2000 or Delta E 2000) is the latest international standard for measuring perceptual color difference, published by the International Commission on Illumination (CIE). It improves on earlier formulas (CIE76, CIE94) by accounting for how the human visual system actually perceives differences in lightness, chroma (saturation), and hue. CIEDE2000 includes corrections for:

- **Lightness weighting** — the human eye is more sensitive to lightness differences than chroma differences
- **Chroma weighting** — differences in saturation are perceived differently at different chroma levels
- **Hue rotation** — the eye is less sensitive to hue differences in the blue region
- **Interactive effects** — lightness, chroma, and hue differences interact with each other perceptually

Paint Color HQ uses CIEDE2000 to compute the perceptual distance between every pair of colors across all 14 brands. This is the same standard used by paint manufacturers, textile companies, and industrial coatings for quality control.

## Delta E Interpretation Guide

| Delta E | Perception | Practical Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| < 1.0 | Virtually identical | Imperceptible to most people. An excellent cross-brand match. |
| 1.0 - 2.0 | Very close | Only noticeable under ideal viewing conditions (side by side, controlled lighting). Suitable substitute for most purposes. |
| 2.0 - 3.0 | Close | A slight difference visible when placed side by side. Still a good match for most home applications. |
| 3.0 - 5.0 | Noticeable | Clearly distinguishable to most observers. May work as an alternative but not a direct substitute. |
| > 5.0 | Different | Obviously different colors. Not a suitable match. |

## What is LRV (Light Reflectance Value)?

LRV measures how much light a painted surface reflects, on a scale of 0 (pure black, absorbs all light) to 100 (pure white, reflects all light). LRV is critical for room planning:

- **LRV 80-100:** Very light colors. Make rooms feel open, airy, and spacious. Ideal for small rooms, ceilings, and north-facing rooms.
- **LRV 50-80:** Medium-light colors. Versatile for most rooms. Provide warmth and depth while keeping the space bright.
- **LRV 30-50:** Medium colors. Add character and coziness. Work best in rooms with good natural light.
- **LRV 10-30:** Dark colors. Create drama and intimacy. Best for accent walls, large rooms, or rooms with abundant light.
- **LRV 0-10:** Very dark colors. Bold and dramatic. Use sparingly or in rooms where you intentionally want a cocoon effect.

**Practical tip:** For rooms with limited natural light (north-facing, basements), choose colors with LRV 55 or higher to keep the space from feeling dark. For rooms with abundant south-facing light, you have more flexibility with lower LRV colors.

## Understanding Undertones

Every paint color has an undertone — a subtle secondary hue beneath the apparent surface color. Undertones are the #1 reason paints "look different on the wall" than expected.

**Warm undertones** include yellow, orange, red, and pink. Colors with warm undertones feel cozy and inviting. They pair well with warm wood tones, brass/gold hardware, and warm lighting.

**Cool undertones** include blue, green, and purple. Colors with cool undertones feel crisp and clean. They pair well with cool-toned flooring (gray tile, white marble), chrome/silver hardware, and cool LED lighting.

**Neutral undertones** are balanced between warm and cool. These are the most versatile and work in the widest range of settings. Examples: Sherwin-Williams Agreeable Gray, Benjamin Moore Revere Pewter.

**How to identify undertones:** Hold a paint chip next to a sheet of pure white paper. The undertone becomes immediately visible. Compare multiple colors in the same family (e.g., three different grays) — the undertone differences jump out when seen side by side.

**Why undertones matter:** Undertones need to harmonize with the fixed elements in your room — flooring, countertops, cabinetry. A warm-toned floor + cool-toned wall paint = visual tension. A warm floor + warm wall = harmony. This is why "gray" paint can look purple on one wall and perfectly neutral on another — it depends on what's surrounding it.

---

# Site Structure Reference

## URL Patterns
- Individual colors: /colors/{brand-slug}/{color-slug}
- Color families: /colors/family/{family-slug}
- Brand pages: /brands/{brand-slug}
- Blog posts: /blog/{post-slug}
- Brand-to-brand matches: /match/{source-brand}/to/{target-brand}
- Tools: /tools/room-visualizer, /tools/paint-calculator, /tools/palette-generator, /tools/color-identifier
- Compare: /compare
- Search: /search

## Brand Slugs
sherwin-williams, benjamin-moore, behr, valspar, ppg, dunn-edwards, farrow-ball, kilz, vista-paint, hirshfields, colorhouse, dutch-boy, ral, mpc

## Color Family Slugs
red, orange, yellow, green, blue, purple, pink, brown, gray, beige, white, off-white, neutral, black
